cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, there are some brothers and sisters in a family. The problem says that every brother has twice as many sisters as brothers. And every sister has one fewer brother than sisters. We need to find out the total number of siblings. First, I need to set up variables. Let me think. Let me denote the number of brothers as B and the number of sisters as S. That seems straightforward. Now, let's look at the first statement: Every brother has twice as many sisters as brothers. Hmm. Wait, so if we take a brother's perspective, how many brothers and sisters does he see? Well, if a brother is looking at his siblings, the number of brothers he has would be B - 1. Because he's a brother himself, so he doesn't count himself. And the number of sisters he has is S, because all the sisters are his sisters. The problem says that every brother has twice as many sisters as brothers. So, the number of sisters (S) is twice the number of brothers (B - 1). So, mathematically, that would be S
